title: 「While 對比句」句型
titleZh: 「While 句——對比兩個人物、群體或情況」
titleEn: Using While to contrast people, groups or situations

source.file: Sentence Sturcture 34 - 「While 句——對比兩個人物、群體或情況」.pdf
omissions: English versions of Chinese-only Important Notes explanations are faithful editorial translations.

formulas.formula: Subject A + verb + information, while Subject B + verb + information.

example: Some people value speed, while others care more about accuracy.
exampleZh: 有些人重視速度，而另一些人則更在意準確性。

examples.en: Some people value speed, while others care more about accuracy.
examples.zh: 有些人重視速度，而另一些人則更在意準確性。
examples.highlight: while others care more about accuracy

meaning.zh: 本課的 while 意思接近 whereas 或 by contrast，用來比較兩個不同的人、群體、事物或情況。

rules.en: In this lesson, while expresses contrast. It means approximately whereas or by contrast and compares different people, groups, things or situations.
rules.zh: 本課的 while 表示「對比」，意思接近 whereas 或 by contrast，用來比較兩個不同的人、群體、事物或情況。
examples: Some students enjoy group work, while others prefer to study alone.

rules.en: Normally put a comma before while when it connects two complete clauses.
rules.zh: 當 while 連接兩個完整句子時，通常在 while 前加逗號。
examples: Mia likes tea, while her brother prefers coffee.

rules.en: Both sides of while should normally be complete clauses with a subject and a verb.
rules.zh: While 兩邊都要有完整子句；while 後面通常需要有主語和動詞。
examples: The first plan saves money, while the second plan saves time.

rules.en: This lesson does not use while to mean ‘during the time that’.
rules.zh: 本句型不包括 while 表示「在……期間／當……的時候」的意思。
examples: I listened to music while I was cooking.

rules.en: Distinguish contrasting while from concessive while. If while can be replaced by whereas, it contrasts two sides; if it can be replaced by although or even though, it expresses concession.
rules.zh: 分辨表示對比與讓步的 while：可以換成 whereas 時表示兩者對比；可以換成 although / even though 時表示「雖然」，屬於讓步。
examples: Some people value speed, while others care more about accuracy.
While natural foods are nutritious, they are often too expensive.

benefits.zh: 把兩項資料放在同一句中，讓讀者立即看出兩者在哪一方面不同。
不需要連續寫兩個短句，也可以自然地比較兩個人物或情況。

benefits.en: It helps you present two sides fairly.
benefits.zh: 使用 while 時，兩個觀點都能得到清楚介紹，適合討論不同選擇、人物需要或社會觀點。

benefits.en: It gives you an alternative to but.
benefits.zh: While 提供另一種自然的對比方式，避免不斷重複使用 but，令句式更多變。

benefits.en: It is useful in speaking and writing.
benefits.zh: 可用於比較兩類人、兩個選擇、兩種方法、兩個地方、兩項研究結果，以及支持者與反對者的看法。

sourceOmissions: The PDF presents the Important Notes mainly in Chinese; their English versions are faithful editorial translations.

en: Rewrite each sentence using while to show a contrast between two people, groups, things or situations.
Use while to mean whereas / by contrast, not although.
The first word of each answer has been provided.
zh: 使用 while 改寫以下句子，以對比兩個人物、群體、事物或情況。
本練習中的 while 應表示「相比之下」，而不是「雖然」。
每題已提供答案的第一個字。

questions.prompt: Some students enjoy group work. Other students prefer to study alone.
questions.promptZh: 有些學生喜歡小組學習。另一些學生則較喜歡獨自溫習。
questions.answer: Some students enjoy group work, while others prefer to study alone.
questions.answerZh: 有些學生喜歡小組學習，而另一些學生則較喜歡獨自溫習。
questions.highlight: Some students enjoy group work, while others prefer to study alone.

questions.prompt: Mia gets up early, but her brother sleeps until late.
questions.promptZh: 米婭很早起床，但她的哥哥睡到很晚。
questions.answer: Mia gets up early, while her brother sleeps until late.

questions.prompt: According to the survey, younger customers preferred mobile payments. Older customers, in contrast, used cash more often.
questions.promptZh: 根據調查，年輕顧客較喜歡流動支付。相比之下，年長顧客則較常使用現金。

questions.prompt: One policy gives families direct financial support, whereas the other invests in public services.
questions.promptZh: 一項政策向家庭提供直接經濟支援，而另一項政策則投資於公共服務。

questions.answer: The northern route is shorter but often crowded, while the southern route takes longer but is usually quieter.
questions.answerZh: 北面路線較短但經常擠塞，而南面路線需要較長時間但通常較清靜。
questions.highlight: The northern route is shorter but often crowded, while the southern route takes longer but is usually quieter.

questions.prompt: Small firms can often make decisions quickly. Large organisations, on the other hand, usually have more specialist staff.
questions.promptZh: 小型公司通常可以迅速作出決定。另一方面，大型機構一般擁有更多專業人員。
questions.answer: Small firms can often make decisions quickly, while large organisations usually have more specialist staff.
questions.answerZh: 小型公司通常可以迅速作出決定，而大型機構則一般擁有更多專業人員。
questions.highlight: Small firms can often make decisions quickly, while large organisations usually have more specialist staff.

questions.prompt: Supporters believe the plan will create jobs. Opponents, however, worry that it will increase traffic.
questions.promptZh: 支持者相信該計劃會創造職位。然而，反對者則擔心它會增加交通流量。
questions.answer: Supporters believe the plan will create jobs, while opponents worry that it will increase traffic.
questions.answerZh: 支持者相信該計劃會創造職位，而反對者則擔心它會增加交通流量。
questions.highlight: Supporters believe the plan will create jobs, while opponents worry that it will increase traffic.

questions.prompt: Public hospitals often treat more complex cases, but private clinics may offer shorter waiting times.
questions.promptZh: 公立醫院通常處理較複雜的病例，但私家診所可能提供較短的輪候時間。
questions.answer: Public hospitals often treat more complex cases, while private clinics may offer shorter waiting times.
questions.answerZh: 公立醫院通常處理較複雜的病例，而私家診所則可能提供較短的輪候時間。
questions.highlight: Public hospitals often treat more complex cases, while private clinics may offer shorter waiting times.

questions.prompt: Customers use different standards when choosing a product: some focus mainly on price, whereas others consider durability and after-sales service.
questions.promptZh: 顧客選擇產品時採用不同標準：有些人主要考慮價格，而另一些人則考慮耐用程度和售後服務。
questions.answer: Customers use different standards when choosing a product: some focus mainly on price, while others consider durability and after-sales service.
questions.answerZh: 顧客選擇產品時採用不同標準：有些人主要考慮價格，而另一些人則考慮耐用程度和售後服務。
questions.highlight: Customers use different standards when choosing a product: some focus mainly on price, while others consider durability and after-sales service.

questions.prompt: The first training method gives learners fixed steps. The second takes a different approach and encourages independent problem-solving.
questions.promptZh: 第一種訓練方法為學習者提供固定步驟。第二種方法採取不同做法，鼓勵學習者獨立解決問題。
questions.answer: The first training method gives learners fixed steps, while the second encourages independent problem-solving.
questions.answerZh: 第一種訓練方法為學習者提供固定步驟，而第二種方法則鼓勵學習者獨立解決問題。
questions.highlight: The first training method gives learners fixed steps, while the second encourages independent problem-solving.

questions.prompt: The older neighbourhood is known for its historic buildings and local shops. The new district, in contrast, offers modern facilities and better transport links.
questions.promptZh: 舊社區以歷史建築和本區商店聞名。相比之下，新區則提供現代化設施和較佳的交通連接。
questions.answer: The older neighbourhood is known for its historic buildings and local shops, while the new district offers modern facilities and better transport links.
questions.answerZh: 舊社區以歷史建築和本區商店聞名，而新區則提供現代化設施和較佳的交通連接。
questions.highlight: The older neighbourhood is known for its historic buildings and local shops, while the new district offers modern facilities and better transport links.

questions.prompt: During a team project, one member may generate creative ideas, whereas another may identify risks and turn those ideas into practical steps.
questions.promptZh: 在團隊項目中，一名成員可能提出創意構思，而另一名成員則可能找出風險，並把這些構思化為實際步驟。
questions.answer: During a team project, one member may generate creative ideas, while another may identify risks and turn those ideas into practical steps.
questions.answerZh: 在團隊項目中，一名成員可能提出創意構思，而另一名成員則可能找出風險，並把這些構思化為實際步驟。
questions.highlight: During a team project, one member may generate creative ideas, while another may identify risks and turn those ideas into practical steps.
